---
title: "surveyreport TODO"
author: "Stephan Daus"
date: "27 3 2021"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

# Overall design
* Each of the add_* functions should call an underlying hidden function that generates the specified output for a single case, and the add_* function will loop across all rows in the design frame with purrr::pmap() and attach the output using a standard helper function. Then an autoreport-function could do looping itself by only calling underlying functions
* class-based objects, or just lists for now? 

# create_df_from_surveyxact
* Remove plyr::dlply

# step_auto_tick_recode
* Some form of automatic recode of multiple tick-box items where no cross is missing? Or perhaps strongly advice people not to use them?

# create_infoframe

# add_percent

# add_regression
* Extract dummy recoder?
# How to distinguish x_var as single latent variable from set of manifest variables?
# Improve extraction of errors in MplusAutomation::readModels(). New separate function: Extract variables that are failing
# V2: Rerun without poor variables if NDependent>4: toupper(colnames(rdata)). Separate function to work on output dataframe.

## run_model 

## prepare_mplus_model

## extract_mplus_model
* Make into broom tidier? Though will be of limited use.
* cronbach, mcdonald_omega? Or using psych::alpha

## prepare_lavaan_model

# add_latent_diff

# add_fit_meaning

# add_effect_meaning

# add_percent_meaning

# add_full_meaning

# add_percentbar_chart

# add_percentbar_plot, add_percentbar_plot

# add_forest_chart, add_forest_plot, add_forest_rvg

# add_bivariate_chart, add_bivariate_plot, add_bivariate_rvg

# create_report_entry

# create_report

# create_report_grouped
* group-specific reports with automatic email sending

# utils
## check_options
## omitted_recoder
## print2.rdocx
* replace utils::zip with officer::unpack_file

## add_element
## loop across (basically just a wrapper to purrr::pmap)


# NAMESPACE

# DESCRIPTION

# Examples and documentation

# Other

parsnip, broom, yardstick extensions for SEM models: 
https://www.tidymodels.org/learn/develop/models/ and https://www.tidymodels.org/learn/develop/broom/ and https://www.tidymodels.org/learn/develop/metrics/
easystats extensions (or not, as they don't yet support tidymodels)
workflow-package extensions
parsnip, and returns broom, yardstick)
